articles.title: US attacks Iran's southern city of Bushehr
articles.summary: US attacks Iran's southern city of Bushehr US strikes several locations in Iran's southern port city of Bushehr, home to the country's civilian nuclear plant, state news media reported. "A few minutes ago, several locations in Bushehr city were hit by American enemy's projectiles," said Bushehr governor Mohammad Mozaffari, adding that the scope and extent of the strikes are under investigation.

articles.title: Kuwaiti air defences responding to Iranian attacks for a second time
articles.summary: Kuwaiti air defences responding to Iranian attacks for a second time Kuwaiti air defences have been activated and are responding to Iranian attacks for a second time in the past few hours.  "The General Staff of the Army confirms that any that any explosions that may be heard are the result of air defense systems intercepting hostile targets," the Kuwaiti Army said in a post on X, urging the public to adhere to security and safety instructions.

articles.title: Iran claims attacks on Kuwait
articles.summary: Iran claims attacks on Kuwait Iran’s Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC) claimed attacks on US military facilities at the Ali Al Salem base in Kuwait. It said in a statement carried by IRIB that a  drone attack "completely destroyed" an early-warning radar system.  Other attacks targeted an aviation equipment warehouse and a hangar housing US MQ-9 drones, setting several aircrafts ablaze.

articles.title: Centcom claims attacks on Iranian command centres, air defence facilities, coastal surveillance
articles.summary: Centcom claims attacks on Iranian command centres, air defence facilities, coastal surveillance  The US Central Command said it completed its ninth consecutive night of strikes against Iran, claiming attacks on Iranian military coastal surveillance and air defence facilities. "CENTCOM assets targeted Iranian military command centers, air defense and coastal surveillance sites, maritime capabilities, missile and drone launch sites, and communications networks," it said in a post on X.

articles.title: IRGC says US aircrafts targeted at Jordan’s Aqaba airport
articles.summary: IRGC says US aircrafts targeted at Jordan’s Aqaba airport Iran’s Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC) claimed attacks targeting US C-17 transport planes and a P-8 aircraft at Aqaba airport in Jordan. According to the statement, the US aircrafts "were targeted with ballistic missiles, causing severe damage to a number of them". The IRGC also said that it targeted 20 shelters where US forces were stationed in the in the Muwaffaq Salti Air Base, "killing of dozens of American terrorist forces".

articles.title: Rubio accuses Iran of using Hormuz Strait as 'leverage against the world'
articles.summary: Rubio accuses Iran of using Hormuz Strait as 'leverage against the world' US Secretary of State Marco Rubio accused Iran of using the Strait of Hormuz as leverage amid the ongoing conflict in the region, calling on other countries to "step up" to protect global shipping. "It's clear that Iran, at least some people in Iran, want to control the straits and hold that as leverage against the world," Rubio told reporters. "The United States will do and continue to do what it needs to, to protect global shipping, but other countries need to begin to step up and provide, whether it's hardware or fina

articles.title: IRGC says Iran targeted two oil tankers in Hormuz Strait
articles.summary: IRGC says Iran targeted two oil tankers in Hormuz Strait The Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC) announced targeting "two offending oil tankers" attempting to travel through the Strait of Hormuz. The IRGC said the vessels were trying to "enter and exit the unsafe and dangerous route" south of the waterway, adding that this was done under the coercion of the US army.  "This is our land, and the intervention of the American terrorist army from thousands of kilometers away has no legal basis and will be dealt with with certainty," the statement read.  "As long as Ame

articles.title: Trump says US hitting Iran 'very hard' to honour soldiers killed
articles.summary: Trump says US hitting Iran 'very hard' to honour soldiers killed US President Donald Trump said that the latest attacks on Iran were carried out in "honour" of US service members recently killed. "We hit them very hard again tonight, and we did that in honour of the, probably three, it's probably three great patriots," he told reporters.  When asked about the military fatalities, Trump responded that "we feel very badly," adding that those who died were fighting so that Tehran "cannot have a nuclear weapon".

articles.title: Iran launches new attacks on 'enemy targets', as ongoing blasts heard south of the country
articles.summary: Iran launches new attacks on 'enemy targets', as ongoing blasts heard south of the country Iranian local media is continuing to report more explosions heard south of the country, including in Bushehr, Sirik and Hormozgan.  Meanwhile, state news agency IRNA reported that a new wave of Iranian missiles were fired from Lorestan towards "enemy targets".

articles.title: Vessel ablaze off the coast of Oman's Kumzar, UKMTO says
articles.summary: Vessel ablaze off the coast of Oman's Kumzar, UKMTO says The United Kingdom Maritime Trade Operations (UKMTO) is reporting that a ship is on fire eight nautical miles north west of Oman's Kumzar.  According to UKMTO, it received information from "military authorities that a vessel is on fire", adding that the cause has yet to be verified.  "Vessels are advised to transit with caution and report any suspicious activity to UKMTO while authorities investigate," it added in its latest update.

articles.title: Iran could 'target unspecified locations' in central Manama, US embassy in Bahrain warns
articles.summary: Iran could 'target unspecified locations' in central Manama, US embassy in Bahrain warns The US Embassy in Bahrain warned in a post on X that Iran "may seek to target unspecified locations in central Manama". It urged American citizens to "remain vigilant, follow local authorities’ instructions, and review the latest guidance" from the US embassy.

articles.title: Iran war live: US military completes ninth night of strikes on Iran
articles.summary: US forces attack Iran for ninth consecutive night as one US soldier killed in northern Iraq.

articles.title: Reports of blasts across Iran
articles.summary: Reports of blasts across Iran Shortly after Centcom announced launching attacks against Iran for the ninth night in a row, several explosions were reported across the country.  Blasts were reported in Iran's southern cities of Bandar Mahshahr, Bandar Imam Khomeini and Chabahar as well as the north western city of Tabriz.  According to Iran's semi-official Mehr news agency,  the country's air defence systems were activated in Konarak.

articles.summary: Gold steadied after the US and Iran ramped up attacks over the weekend, as traders assess the likelihood of the Federal Reserve raising interest rates to contain inflation.

articles.title: Centcom begins new wave of attacks on Iran for ninth consecutive night
articles.summary: Centcom begins new wave of attacks on Iran for ninth consecutive night The US Central Command said it began "conducting a new wave of strikes against Iran" for the ninth consecutive night. "The strikes will continue degrading Iranian military capabilities used to attack commercial vessels and civilian mariners transiting the Strait of Hormuz," it said in a statement posted on X.

articles.title: IRGC claims 'surprise attack' on command centre in Syria
articles.summary: IRGC claims 'surprise attack' on command centre in Syria Iran's Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps announced they launched a "surprise attack" on a command centre in Syria's al-Tanf, in a statement carried by state news agency IRNA. They said that the assault was carried out as "revenge for the blood of martyred soldiers" killed in Iran's south eastern city of Iranshahr.
